reviews.title: Not worth to stay hotel
reviews.text:
This is my honest opinion. The service at this hotel was very disappointing. When my girlfriend arrived with her child and had multiple bags, she had to carry everything up the stairs to the lobby by herself. Not a single staff member offered to help, which was very disappointing.
As for the room, it did not match the photos shown online. While the pictures made it look clean and presentable, the actual room was untidy. The bed looked poorly prepared, with messy sheets that appeared slightly yellowish, making it seem unclean. The pillows also felt old and uncomfortable.
Overall, the room did not feel welcoming or well-maintained. The wooden floor planks even appeared to be peeling off in some areas.
I would advise future guests not to rely too much on the photos online. I hope the hotel management will take this feedback seriously and improve both their service and room conditions to better meet guests’ expectations.

reviews.title: Pick somewhere else
reviews.text: The “gym facilities” are a broken treadmill and a weight machine that is also broken. The pool has multiple exposed wires in the water from where light installations were supposed to be installed? Concerning that all it would take is someone flipping the wrong switch and the pool would be a death trap. The front of house had one person who was friendly and one who went out of her way to make me not want to talk with them again. The shower door was shattered, glass pieces fell into the shower while I was trying to shower. The whole floor stank of cigarettes. Honestly I’m not mad, you get what you pay for, and it was certainly very cheap. But I’ve been staying in hotels across Vietnam for the past 6 weeks and this is my first negative review.

reviews.title: Nostalgia Hotel, more like Nostalgia Motel: The Sequel Nobody Greenlit.
reviews.text:
Look, staying here wasn’t exactly a vacation. It was more like being trapped in a time capsule from 1984 that someone forgot to clean or fix… ever. The hotel isn’t terrible, per se, it just needs a therapist and a complete renovation.

The facilities? Basically imaginary.

The pool? Looks like it’s starring in a post-apocalyptic movie.

The “fitness center” must be hiding in Narnia because it sure isn’t here.

Restaurant? Nope. Hope you like instant noodles and regret.

Rooftop access? Denied. As if you're going to sneak up there and realize how sad the view is.

It’s definitely leaning into “future love hotel” territory, vibe says passion, décor says mildew.

Now, to be fair, for the price? It’s kind of unbeatable. The rooms aren't sparkling clean, but they’re just clean enough to crash in and not lose your will to live.

But seriously, stop advertising like you’re a legit, full-service hotel. You’re not. You're the budget bin of accommodations. Manage expectations, and maybe we’ll stop expecting towel swans and working amenities.

reviews.title: Should have never been listed on Agoda
reviews.text: A dated hotel in a crappy location in a squalid and trash-strewn neighborhood. Rooms were cleaned ok but furniture and towels were disgustingly stained. The neighboring school is loud with drumming and screaming from early morning on some days. Should have never been listed in Agoda, but is listed under at least 2 different names in order to be confused with much better reviewed hotels, leading to my booking screwup. The epitome of communist luxury. Couldn’t wait to check out! Avoid.

reviews.title: Not good
reviews.text:
have booked this hotel more than 10 times in the past because I also work as a travel agent and often bring guests from my country. This time, I booked it for my own family, expecting the same quality as before. Unfortunately, this stay was very disappointing.

When my family first arrived, the check-in was fine and the room seemed clean. However, after two days my mother told me that the towels had not been changed since check-in. I was surprised because the room was being cleaned every day. When we asked the reception, they said it was because the “Do Not Disturb” sign was on the door, but that wasn’t the case during the two days when the cleaners entered.

Then a storm came to Da Nang, and the elevator stopped working. My grandparents are 76 years old and we were on the 6th floor. They could not walk up the stairs, so they had to wait in the lobby for hours. When I asked the staff to fix the elevator, they said it was a government issue and they couldn’t help. I asked for another solution, like moving us to a lower floor, but the staff said we had to pay extra for a different room, even though the situation wasn’t our fault.

We eventually had to help my grandparents slowly walk upstairs, resting on chairs along the way. On top of that, the hot water stopped working for three days. Each day, the staff told us to wait until the next day because they didn’t have the parts to fix it, and they couldn’t say when it would be repaired.

We decided to check out three days earlier than planned because we didn’t want to keep my family in such uncomfortable conditions. To make things worse, at checkout they even charged us 20,000 VND for two bottles of water in the room.

Overall, the staff showed very little care for their customers, and the service was far below expectations. I will not recommend this hotel to anyone, especially for families or elderly guests. We moved to another hotel afterward, and it had everything working properly.

reviews.title: Close to the local market
reviews.text: This place is close to the local market where you can get fresh fruits, veggies and also decent bread & cookies from a Russian bakery. The lobby area looks alright though the building itself feels pretty run down. There's a leak in the bathroom and a drain issue in the shower so the water flows onto the floor. The wifi was finicky and the walls so thin you can hear laughters and chats from the next room. While the staff was very friendly, they weren't much help with the troubling issues unfortunately. We actually came here last minute due to a double booking issue at our original place but had to find a new place after two nights.

reviews.title: It's good for the price.
reviews.text:
Taxi fare for 4 people  
Mikibichi is about 60,000 to 80,000 VND round trip via Indrive.  
Lotte Mart is about 50,000 VND round trip via Indrive.  
Han Market is about 80,000 to 90,000 VND round trip.  

For this price, there’s also a pool, and it’s a good value for the price.  
The staff was sleeping on the sofa in the early morning.  
I arrived early in the morning, but the check-in went smoothly, and they were kind.  

On the last day, I encountered a lizard in the room.  
It was cute~~  

What I found disappointing was that there weren't many places to eat near the accommodation.  
It was good that the Park My An Market was within walking distance,  
and I was able to withdraw money without any fees from the VIDB bank on the way to the market.  

Overall, it was a good value for the price.

The 'fitness centre is only 2 fitness machines next to the pool. There was a dead gecko in the pool so we didn't use it. One of our beds did not have a duvet, just an empty cover. We asked for one but they didn't have any in the size needed so we got a much bigger one that didn't fit the cover. 
Apart from that the rooms are good size, beds are comfy and it's walking distance to the beach and restaurants  which makes it a good option for the price.
